title: Give it another year
excerpt: These nine players should play college hoops next season. Here's why. Plus: Christian Anderson and Allen Graves stay in the draft, Matt Able withdraws, transfer portal news, and more.
author: Sean Paul

title: Back to school
excerpt: Kentucky, Louisville and Duke all get good news with NBA Draft decisions. Plus: Oklahoma State lands a scoring forward, more transfer moves, NC State's big international addition, and much more.
author: Mike Miller

title: Big is back
excerpt: Chinyelu will return to Florida for senior season, giving Gators nation's top frontcourt. Plus: Players Era released 2026 brackets, Cincinnati landed an elite local prospect, and much more.
author: Tristan Freeman

title: A scheduling gem
excerpt: The 'Diamond Cup' will feature some of college hoops' elite programs starting in 2027-28. Plus: UNLV's 4-star addition, recruiting news, Will Wade's latest international add, and much more.
author: Samuel Lance

title: Is LSU going too far?
excerpt: Will Wade's program reportedly bringing in three more former professional players for its 2026-27 roster, drawing plenty of criticism. Plus: USC withdraws from NBA Draft, transfer portal moves.
author: Sean Paul

title: Bold Bayou move?
excerpt: LSU reportedly trying to sign a player who has been a pro for six years. Plus: Clemson makes another smart roster move, Arizona and Michigan plan foreign trips, Sac State retains its scoring star.
author: Mike Miller

title: Center of attention
excerpt: How has Lewis Uvwo emerged as one of the top big man prospects? We talked to him about it. Plus: Purdue added a 7-footer, St. John's brought in a prep player, Texas and Auburn got international players.
author: Samuel Lance

title: All quiet on the portal front
excerpt: The transfer portal has slowed down — but don't be fooled. The biggest moves are still coming. A breakdown of what's left and who's still available.
author: Tristan Freeman

title: The portal is closed
excerpt: The spring transfer portal window is officially closed. Here's a full recap of the biggest moves, the winners, the losers, and what it means heading into next season.
author: Mike Miller

title: Top target destinations
excerpt: Where are the best available players in the transfer portal considering? Plus: Texas Tech lands CAA POY, Boston College has a big day, Cayden Boozer heading back to Duke and much more.
author: Tristan Freeman

title: The weekend in portal hoops
excerpt: Louisville's the early leader when it comes to signing transfers in Bidunga and Shelstad. Plus moves from Houston, Iowa State, Florida, Auburn, Michigan and more.
author: Mike Miller

title: Portal prizes, Top 100 rankings
excerpt: Michigan, Providence add big pieces to 2026-27 rosters. Plus: We rank the Top 100 players in the portal, highlight notable entrants, and NBA decisions for Houston and Stanford.
author: Tristan Freeman

title: Bold move
excerpt: New North Carolina coach Michael Malone started his roster revamp with an experienced PG instead of an incoming freshman. Plus: Indiana adds three transfers, Nebraska retains an All-Big Ten player.
author: Mike Miller

title: Coaching carousel speeds up
excerpt: Creighton starts a new era, Arizona State and Cincinnati adjust to life in the Big 12, while UNC weighs its future with Hubert Davis. Plus: NIT basketball and a rundown of planned portal activity.
author: Mike Miller

title: Eligibility chaos coming?
excerpt: NCAA considering a new blanket 5-year eligibility rule that could upend current roster planning. May could be wild. Plus: Texas Tech has another backcourt scorer, more portal moves, NBA Draft decisions.
author: Tristan Freeman

title: Playing GM in a booming NIL market
excerpt: How much is the NIL market spike? Some elite programs are busy weighing their next moves. Plus: Top programs cash in on retention, the Big East surges, latest portal activity.
author: Samuel Lance

title: Self return
excerpt: Bill Self announces he'll return with 'renewed clarity' for a 24th season at Kansas. Plus: Tennessee benefits from early player movement, Murray State's player retention, and Tom Izzo's surprising revelation.
author: Samuel Lance
